sections.title: May Have — Introduction and Formula
sections.explanation: May Have is an essential building block of spoken and written English. Formula: Subject + may have + past participle (uncertain possibility about the past). Rules: 'May have' expresses uncertainty about something that might have happened in the past | Formula: may have + V3 (past participle) | Weaker certainty than 'must have' | Negative: may not have + V3
sections.formula: Subject + may have + past participle (uncertain possibility about the past)

examples.hindi: वह शायद वहाँ गया होगा।
शायद उसने यह किताब पढ़ी होगी।
examples.english: She may have read this book.

examples.hindi: शायद उन्होंने मुझे फोन किया होगा।
शायद वह गलती की होगी।
examples.english: He may have made a mistake.

examples.hindi: शायद वह भूल गई होगी।
शायद उन्होंने यह देखा होगा।

sections.title: May Have — Daily Life Usage
sections.explanation: Everyday sentences using May Have for daily conversation.
sections.formula: Subject + may have + past participle (uncertain possibility about the past)

examples.hindi: शायद उसने सच बोला होगा।
examples.english: He may have told the truth.

examples.hindi: शायद वह देर से पहुँची होगी।
शायद हमने गलत रास्ता चुना होगा।
examples.english: We may have chosen the wrong path.

examples.hindi: शायद उसने मुझसे झूठ बोला होगा।
examples.english: He may not have told me the truth.

examples.hindi: शायद वह बीमार रही होगी।
शायद उन्होंने मेरा message नहीं देखा होगा।
examples.english: They may not have seen my message.

sections.title: May Have — Office & Professional Usage
sections.hindiTitle: May Have — ऑफिस और पेशेवर उपयोग
sections.explanation: Practice May Have in professional and office contexts.
sections.formula: Subject + may have + past participle (uncertain possibility about the past)

examples.hindi: शायद वह इसे भूल गया होगा।
शायद वह पहले ही जा चुकी होगी।
शायद उन्होंने decision बदल दिया होगा।
examples.english: They may have changed the decision.

examples.hindi: शायद उसने मुझे पहचान लिया होगा।

sections.title: May Have — Negative and Question Forms
sections.hindiTitle: May Have — नकारात्मक और प्रश्न रूप
sections.explanation: How to make negative sentences and questions using May Have. Common mistakes: wrong verb form after the modal, or missing "have" in perfect modals.
sections.formula: Subject + may have + past participle (uncertain possibility about the past)

examples.hindi: शायद वह वहाँ नहीं गई होगी।
examples.english: She may not have gone there.

examples.hindi: शायद उन्होंने सही किया होगा।
examples.english: They may have done it right.

examples.hindi: शायद उसने मुझे बताया होगा।
शायद वह थक गई होगी।
